product_overview: Text2Form.ai is an AI-first form and quiz builder that converts plain-language prompts (and optional documents) into ready-to-use web forms, shareable public links, and analytics dashboards. It supports guest creation and Supabase-backed authenticated users with email and Google sign-in.
core_goals: Allow anyone to generate a structured form or quiz from a short natural-language prompt (optionally supplemented with files) in seconds.
Support both guest-created forms (claimable later) and authenticated user ownership with a personal dashboard for management and analytics.
Provide a simple, testable public form experience with secure handling of quiz answers and real-time submission tracking.

features.name: AI-Powered Form Generation
features.description: Create a structured form/quiz from a natural-language prompt and optional files; stores the form and redirects to the editor.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type prompt in textarea (data-testid="prompt-input") -> (optional) Attach up to 3 files via FileUploader -> Click Create button (data-testid="generate-button") -> Await API response from /api/generate -> Redirect to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> See generated fields rendered in editor

Navigate to / -> Click Create button without entering a prompt and without attaching files -> See generation error message (data-testid="generation-error") from API (400) informing 'Please provide a prompt or upload a file for context.'

features.name: Email Authentication (Sign Up / Sign In)
features.description: Users can sign up or sign in with email and password on the centralized login page; sessions redirect to the dashboard on success.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Enter email address -> Enter password -> Click Sign In or Sign Up -> Immediate login after successful credential validation -> Redirect to /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Enter email address -> Enter password -> Click Sign In or Sign Up -> Authentication fails (invalid credentials) -> See error toast/message and remain on /login

features.name: Google OAuth Authentication (Claiming Support)
features.description: Users may sign in with Google; OAuth callback handles session creation and optional guest form claiming via claim_id.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Click Google Sign-In button -> Browser redirects to Google OAuth -> OAuth provider returns to /auth/callback -> Exchange code and create session -> If claim_id present update form owner -> Redirect to /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Click Google Sign-In button -> OAuth fails or is blocked -> /auth/callback redirects to /?error=auth-callback-failed -> See error notice on home page

features.name: File Upload System (Context for Generation)
features.description: Drag-and-drop or select files (PDF/DOCX/TXT/PNG/JPG) on the home page to provide document context to the AI generator.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Drag & drop or select supported files (PDF, DOCX, TXT, PNG, JPG) using FileUploader -> See file preview cards and file count -> Click Create button -> Files uploaded as multipart/form-data to /api/generate -> Redirect to /dashboard/[id]/edit

Navigate to / -> Attempt to upload unsupported file type (e.g., .exe) or exceed 3 files -> FileUploader rejects the file and shows validation message -> User removes invalid files or reduces the count

features.name: Form Editor
features.description: Edit generated forms: change title/description, adjust fields (labels, placeholders, types, options), reorder, delete, add new questions, toggle quiz mode, and save.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit -> Edit Title field -> Edit question label and placeholder -> Change question type via dropdown -> Toggle Required checkbox -> Reorder questions via move controls -> Click Save Form -> If form.user_id is null redirect to /f/[id]?success=true -> If authenticated owner redirect to /dashboard/[id]
Navigate to /dashboard/[id]/edit for a form owned by another user -> See toast 'Access denied' -> Redirect to /dashboard

features.name: Dynamic Form Rendering (Frontend)
features.description: Render a form schema into interactive inputs (text, textarea, radio, checkbox, select) with validation, animated entry, and accessible test IDs.
user_flows: Navigate to /f/[id] -> See form title (data-testid="form-title") and description -> Interact with individual fields (type into input-{id}, open select-{id}, click radio-{id}-{idx}, toggle checkbox-{id}-{idx}) -> Click submit-form-button -> See loading spinner -> Receive submission response and view Thank You or quiz score
Navigate to /f/[id] -> Leave a required field empty -> Click submit-form-button -> See inline validation error banner for the required field and submission prevented

features.name: Public Form Page (Fill & Submit)
features.description: Respondents open /f/[id], fill fields, and submit; submissions are stored and quizzes are auto-graded when applicable.
user_flows: Navigate to /f/[id] -> Fill all required fields -> Click submit-form-button -> POST to /api/submit with { formId, answers } -> Receive 200 OK with success and optional score -> See 'Thank You' or 'X / Y' quiz score
Navigate to /f/invalid-id -> API returns 'Form not found' -> See styled form not found error with 'Back to Home' link

features.name: Analytics Dashboard (Form Details & Charts)
features.description: Owners view analytics at /dashboard/[id]: response counts, average score (quizzes), distribution chart for first selectable field, and a submissions table.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/[id] while authenticated as owner -> See header with form title and description -> View stat cards (stat-total-responses, stat-total-fields) -> View AnalyticsChart (analytics-chart) and SubmissionsTable (submissions-table) -> Click view-form-link to open /f/[id]
Navigate to /dashboard/[id] while not the owner -> See Access Denied state with lock icon and explanatory message -> No analytics shown

features.name: Dashboard Index (My Vault)
features.description: Authenticated users visit /dashboard to see a responsive grid of owned forms, create new forms, and access actions (view, edit, delete, share).
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard while authenticated -> See list of form cards with response counts, badges, edit and view actions -> Click '+ New Form' -> Redirect to /
Navigate to /dashboard while unauthenticated -> Server-side redirect to / -> See home page

features.name: Form Deletion
features.description: Owners can delete their forms from the dashboard by clicking the trash icon and confirming via a browser prompt; deletion calls the delete API.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Click trash icon on a form card -> Confirm deletion via browser prompt -> API POST /api/forms/delete returns success -> Form card is removed and success toast is shown

Navigate to /dashboard -> Click trash icon on a form card while not authenticated or not the owner -> Confirm deletion -> API returns 401/403 -> Show error toast 'Unauthorized' and keep form card present

features.name: Clipboard Sharing (Copy Link)
features.description: Copy a form's public URL from the dashboard or public form page to the clipboard with visual 'Copied!' feedback.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/[id] -> Click copy-link-button -> Copy {origin}/f/{id} to clipboard -> Show 'Copied!' feedback for 2 seconds
Navigate to /f/[id] -> Click Share Link button -> Browser denies clipboard write (permission) -> Show error toast and fallback to manual copy UI

features.name: Toast Notifications
features.description: Global toast system surfaces save successes, access errors, and dynamic API error messages across the app.
user_flows: Perform a save action (e.g., Save Form in editor) -> API returns success -> Show toast 'Form saved successfully' -> Continue on redirected page
Trigger an access or save error (e.g., unauthorized update or DB failure) -> API returns error -> Show destructive toast with the error message -> User remains on current page

routes.description: Landing page with prompt input for form generation
Public interface for filling out and submitting forms

features.name: Email Authentication
features.description: Sign up and sign in using email and password
user_interactions: Click Sign In or Sign Up
Immediate login after successful credential validation (no confirmation needed)

features.name: Form Generation
features.description: Generate dynamic forms from text prompts

features.name: Form Deletion
features.description: Delete owned forms from the dashboard
user_interactions: Click trash icon on a form card

known_limitations.issue: Google OAuth is not testable in automated headless environments
known_limitations.impact: Tests should focus on email/password authentication instead
